recipes.title: Chilli Crab
recipes.description: Singaporean mud crab in a sweet, tangy and mildly spicy tomato chilli gravy thickened with egg.
steps: Clean and chop crabs into pieces, cracking the claws.
Pound garlic, ginger, shallots and chillies into a paste.
Heat oil in a wok and fry the paste until fragrant and slightly oily.
Add tomato sauce, chilli sauce, sambal, sugar, vinegar and water; bring to a simmer.
Add crab pieces, cover and cook 8 minutes until shells are bright red.
Stir cornstarch with water, drizzle in to thicken sauce.
Beat eggs and stream into the sauce, swirling for ribbons.
Garnish with cilantro and serve with mantou buns to mop up the gravy.

recipes.title: Hainanese Chicken Rice
recipes.description: Singapore's beloved poached chicken with fragrant chicken-fat rice, chilli sauce and dark soy.
steps: Stuff chicken with smashed ginger, garlic and scallions, then rub with salt.
Bring a deep pot of water to a rolling boil and dunk chicken three times to set the skin.
Lower heat to a bare simmer and poach chicken for 35 minutes until just cooked.
Plunge chicken into ice water for 10 minutes, then drain and rub with sesame oil.
Saute minced ginger and garlic in oil, add rinsed rice and toast briefly.
Add hot poaching stock and pandan leaves, cook covered until rice is fluffy.
Blend chillies, garlic, ginger, lime and a little stock for chilli sauce.
Chop chicken and serve with rice, cucumber, chilli sauce and dark soy.

recipes.title: Char Kway Teow
recipes.description: Wok-fried flat rice noodles with prawns, cockles, Chinese sausage, egg and bean sprouts in dark soy.
steps: Heat a wok until smoking and melt lard.
Stir-fry minced garlic and sambal, add Chinese sausage and shrimp.
Push aside and crack in eggs, scrambling lightly.
Add noodles and toss with dark and light soy sauce until evenly colored.
Add cockles, bean sprouts and garlic chives, toss 30 seconds for wok hei.
Plate immediately while ingredients are hot.

recipes.title: Laksa
recipes.description: Singaporean Katong-style coconut prawn laksa with thick rice noodles, fish cake and laksa leaves.
steps: Soak dried chillies and blend with shallots, garlic, lemongrass, galangal, candlenuts and belacan.
Saute the laksa paste in oil for 15 minutes until red and fragrant.
Pour in shrimp stock and simmer 10 minutes.
Add coconut milk and tofu puffs, simmer gently for another 5 minutes.
Add shrimp, cockles and fish cake; cook just until shrimp turns pink.
Cook noodles, drain and divide between bowls with bean sprouts.
Ladle hot laksa over and finish with chopped laksa leaves and lime.

recipes.title: Roti Prata
recipes.description: Singaporean flaky pan-fried flatbread with crisp edges, served with curry dipping sauce.
steps: Mix flour, salt, sugar, condensed milk, egg and water into a soft dough; knead 10 minutes.
Divide into balls, coat in oil and rest in a covered tray for 4 hours.
Brown mutton chunks, then simmer with onion, curry powder and coconut milk until tender.
Oil a clean surface and slap a dough ball flat, then flick and stretch tissue-thin.
Brush with ghee, fold into a square and pan-cook on a hot oiled griddle until golden.
Crush each cooked prata gently to fluff layers and serve with hot mutton curry.

recipes.title: Bak Kut Teh
recipes.description: Singaporean pork rib soup simmered with garlic, white pepper, herbs and dark soy.
steps: Parboil pork ribs for 5 minutes, then drain and rinse.
Tie peppercorns, star anise, cinnamon and cloves in a muslin bag.
Place ribs, whole garlic heads and the spice bag in fresh water and bring to a boil.
Lower heat and simmer for 1.5 hours until ribs are tender.
Add dark soy, light soy and salt; simmer 10 more minutes.
Add tofu puffs, enoki mushrooms and lettuce; cook just until wilted.
Serve hot with steamed rice and dark soy with sliced chillies on the side.

recipes.title: Kaya Toast
recipes.description: Singaporean breakfast of crisp toast layered with cold butter and pandan-coconut kaya jam.
steps: Tie pandan leaves into a knot and crush slightly to release aroma.
Whisk eggs and sugar over a double boiler until pale.
Add coconut milk and pandan, then whisk constantly over low heat for 30 minutes until thick and amber.
Remove pandan, cool kaya completely; refrigerate to set.
Toast bread until golden and crisp, then split each slice horizontally.
Spread cold butter on the cut side, top with kaya, sandwich and slice into fingers.
Serve with soft-boiled eggs seasoned with soy and pepper alongside.

recipes.title: Chai Tow Kway
recipes.description: Singaporean stir-fried radish cake with eggs, preserved radish, garlic and dark sweet soy sauce.
steps: Grate daikon and simmer in water until softened.
Whisk rice flour and cornstarch with cool water until smooth, then stream into the daikon.
Stir constantly until thick and shiny, then pour into an oiled pan and steam 45 minutes until firm.
Cool the radish cake completely, then dice into 2cm cubes.
Heat oil in a wok, fry garlic and preserved radish until fragrant.
Add radish cake cubes and pan-fry until edges crisp.
Push aside, scramble eggs in the open space, mix with cake.
Drizzle with dark soy, light soy and sambal; toss with scallions and pepper before serving.

recipes.title: Black Pepper Crab
recipes.description: Singaporean wok-tossed crab in a fragrant black pepper, butter and curry leaf glaze.
steps: Clean and chop crabs into pieces, cracking the claws.
Toast peppercorns until fragrant, then grind coarsely.
Lightly fry crab pieces in oil for 3 minutes to set the shell color.
Wipe wok and melt butter; saute garlic, shallots, curry leaves and chillies until fragrant.
Add ground pepper and cook 1 minute until very aromatic.
Add oyster sauce, light soy, sugar and stock; simmer to a glossy glaze.
Return crab and toss for 5 minutes until coated and cooked through, then serve immediately.
